food_name: Vietnamese Beef Stew with Baguette (Bò Kho Bánh Mì)
aliases: Beef stew with bread (Bò kho bánh mì)

english: Beef stew with bread (Bò kho bánh mì)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'beef stew' class (FOODON_03315595); no specific Vietnamese Bò Kho entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Beef stew — cooked composite dish with vegetables
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers cooked beef stew dishes; no Bò Kho-specific code exists. Closest parent match applied.

serving.description: 1 bowl of beef stew with 1–2 pieces of baguette
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is approximately 600 g, comprising one bowl of slow-cooked beef stew (roughly 450 g) with one to two pieces of Vietnamese baguette (bánh mì, roughly 150 g) for dipping.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Beef stew with vegetables (FDC ID 170720) and French bread/baguette (FDC ID 174924); composite estimate weighted by typical Bò Kho recipe proportions (75% stew, 25% bread by mass)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Bò Kho Bánh Mì entry exists in HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for beef stew with vegetables (FDC ID 170720) and French baguette (FDC ID 174924), weighted at approximately 75% stew and 25% bread by mass, consistent with the 600 g default portion (approximately 450 g stew plus 150 g bread). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. Input ai_generated values (confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this composite and found broadly consistent; the USDA-derived composite is used as the authoritative basis. Sodium is elevated relative to plain beef stew due to fish sauce; the 300 mg per 100 g figure from the input is retained as plausible for a fish-sauce-seasoned Vietnamese stew and is consistent with published Vietnamese dish sodium ranges in FAO/INFOODS SEA.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021: a systematic review. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition. 2021;114(5):1625–1632.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bò Kho Bánh Mì in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI estimated as a weighted composite using values from Atkinson et al. 2021 (peer-reviewed journal, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition): beef stew with potato (GI approximately 65, medium category) weighted at 75% and white baguette (GI approximately 75, high category) weighted at 25%, yielding a blended GI of approximately 65 (medium). Glycemic load per 600 g serving calculated as (65 × 10.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 600 g divided by 100) divided by 100 equals 42.1 g GL. This represents a high GL per full serving, consistent with the large portion size. Atkinson et al. 2021 is a peer-reviewed systematic review published in the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition and constitutes an approved GI source under Project Veritas v2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100 g; a 600 g serving delivers approximately 1800 mg sodium, representing 78–90% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a bowl, request reduced fish sauce during preparation, and choose a wholegrain baguette to improve the potassium-to-sodium ratio and fibre intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL approximately 42) driven by white baguette (GI approximately 75) and potato starch; carbohydrate 10.8 g per 100 g with only 1.3 g dietary fibre per 100 g results in rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the baguette portion to one small piece, substitute wholegrain bread where possible, and increase the vegetable-to-potato ratio in the stew to lower the overall glycemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 2.5 g per 100 g from beef and cooking oil; sodium 300 mg per 100 g from fish sauce; trans fat 0.08 g per 100 g is low but present; a full 600 g serving contributes approximately 15 g saturated fat and 1800 mg sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit the serving to 300–400 g, request lean beef cuts and reduced fish sauce, and pair with a high-fibre wholegrain baguette to moderate saturated fat and sodium intake per meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (beef) is a source of arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways; tomato paste and potatoes are nightshade foods that may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise red meat consumption; if consuming Bò Kho, request a smaller beef portion, retain anti-inflammatory spices (lemongrass, ginger, cinnamon), and monitor joint symptoms after nightshade exposure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bò Kho Bánh Mì is a hearty Vietnamese slow-cooked beef stew served with a crusty Vietnamese baguette (bánh mì) for dipping. The stew is prepared by braising beef brisket or chuck with lemongrass, ginger, cinnamon, star anise, garlic, shallots, tomato paste, fish sauce, carrots, and potatoes over low heat for one to two hours until the beef is tender and the broth is deeply aromatic and slightly thickened. The dish originates from Vietnam and reflects French colonial culinary influence through the use of the baguette. The flavour profile is rich, savoury, and warmly spiced, with a slightly sweet undertone from carrots and a umami depth from fish sauce. The texture of the beef is fall-apart tender, the broth is silky, and the baguette provides a crisp exterior that softens when dipped. Nutritionally, a 600 g serving provides approximately 750 kcal, 45 g protein, 35 g fat, and 65 g carbohydrate. Sodium is elevated at approximately 1800 mg per serving due to fish sauce, making portion control important for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per full serving is high (GL approximately 42), driven primarily by the white baguette and potato content, which is a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or fish allergies. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or lunch dish in Vietnam and is widely available in Vietnamese restaurants across Southeast Asia and Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact Bò Kho Bánh Mì entry exists in any approved primary regional database (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite of beef stew with vegetables (FDC ID 170720) and French baguette (FDC ID 174924), weighted by typical recipe proportions. The input ai_generated values (confidence 0.7) were cross-checked and found broadly consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2021). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite Vietnamese dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026-04-17. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite dish are not reported in the approved sources consulted (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FAO/INFOODS SEA). 3. potassium_mg (210 mg per 100 g) is retained from the input record as plausible for a beef-and-vegetable stew and is consistent with USDA SR Legacy beef stew data. 4. The input record lists two default portions (600 g and 650 g); 600 g is adopted as the primary default serving size as it corresponds to the more specific label. 5. is_halal is set to false as halal certification status cannot be confirmed from available data; consumers should verify with the specific vendor. 6. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on Atkinson et al. 2021 (American Journal of Clinical Nutrition); no direct GI measurement for Bò Kho Bánh Mì was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2026-04-17. 7. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (approved secondary global source per Project Veritas v2 source policy, section 5.1); this is the highest-priority approved source available given the absence of this dish in HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, and FAO/INFOODS SEA databases.
